volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1 - Section 87b)

sections.label: Section 1
sections.original: ὀρθὴν κελεύεις , ᾗ τὸ δένδρον φαίνεται ;
sections.translation: Do you think I should walk straight for yon tree ?

sections.label: Section 2
sections.original: διαρραγείης · ἥδε δʼ αὖ κρώζει πάλιν .
sections.translation: Cursed beast , what are you croaking to me ? . . . to retrace my steps ?

sections.label: Section 3
sections.original: τί ὦ πόνηρʼ ἄνω κάτω πλανύττομεν ;
sections.translation: Why , you wretch , we are wandering at random , we are exerting ourselves only to return to the same spot ; we ' re wasting our time .

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original: τὸ δʼ ἐμὲ κορώνῃ πειθόμενον τὸν ἄθλιον
sections.translation: To think that I should trust to this crow , which has made me cover more than a thousand furlongs !

sections.label: Section 7
sections.original: τὸ δʼ ἐμὲ κολοιῷ πειθόμενον τὸν δύσμορον
sections.translation: And that I , in obedience to this jay , should have worn my toes down to the nails !

sections.label: Section 9
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐδʼ ὅπου γῆς ἐσμὲν οἶδʼ ἔγωγʼ ἔτι .
sections.translation: If only I knew where we were . . .

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original: ἐντευθενὶ τὴν πατρίδʼ ἂν ἐξεύροις σύ που ;
sections.translation: Could you find your country again from here ?

sections.label: Section 11
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἂν μὰ Δία γʼ ἐντεῦθεν Ἐξηκεστίδης .
sections.translation: No , I feel quite sure I could not , any more than could Execestides find his .

sections.label: Section 12
sections.original: οἴμοι .
sections.translation: Alas !

sections.label: Section 12b
sections.original: σὺ μὲν ὦ τᾶν τὴν ὁδὸν ταύτην ἴθι .
sections.translation: Aye , aye , my friend , it ' s surely the road of alases we are following .

sections.label: Section 13
sections.original: ἦ δεινὰ νὼ δέδρακεν οὑκ τῶν ὀρνέων ,
sections.translation: That Philocrates , the bird-seller , played us a scurvy trick ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original: ὃς τώδʼ ἔφασκε νῷν φράσειν τὸν Τηρέα
sections.translation: when he pretended these two guides could help us to find Tereus , the Epops , who is a bird , without being born of one . He has indeed sold us this jay , a true son of Tharrhelides , for an obolus , and this crow for three , but what can they do ? Why , nothing whatever but bite and scratch !

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original: καὶ νῦν τί κέχηνας ; ἔσθʼ ὅποι κατὰ τῶν πετρῶν
sections.translation: What ' s the matter with you then , that you keep opening your beak ? Do you want us to fling ourselves headlong down these rocks ? There is no road that way .

sections.label: Section 22b
sections.original: οὐδὲ μὰ Δίʼ ἐνταῦθά γʼ ἀτραπὸς οὐδαμοῦ .
sections.translation: Not even the vestige of a trail in any direction

sections.label: Section 23
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἡ κορώνη τῆς ὁδοῦ τι λέγει πέρι ;
sections.translation: And what does the crow say about the road to follow ?

sections.label: Section 24
sections.original: οὐ ταὐτὰ κρώζει μὰ Δία νῦν τε καὶ τότε .
sections.translation: By Zeus , it no longer croaks the same thing it did .

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original: τί δὴ λέγει περὶ τῆς ὁδοῦ ;
sections.translation: And which way does it tell us to go now ?

sections.label: Section 25b
sections.original: τί δʼ ἄλλο γʼ ἢ
sections.translation: It says that , by dint of gnawing , it will devour my fingers .

sections.label: Section 27
sections.original: οὐ δεινὸν οὖν δῆτʼ ἐστὶν ἡμᾶς δεομένους
sections.translation: What misfortune is ours ! we strain every nerve to get to the crows , do everything we can to that end , and we cannot find our way !

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original: ἡμεῖς γάρ , ὦνδρες οἱ παρόντες ἐν λόγῳ ,
sections.translation: Yes , spectators , our madness is quite different from that of Sacas . He is not a citizen , and would fain be one at any cost ; we , on the contrary , born of an honorable tribe and family and living in the midst of our fellow-citizens ,

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original: ἀνεπτόμεσθʼ ἐκ τῆς πατρίδος ἀμφοῖν ποδοῖν ,
sections.translation: we have fled from our country as hard as ever we could go . It ' s not that we hate it ; we recognize it to be great and rich , likewise that everyone has the right to ruin himself paying taxes ; but the crickets only chirrup among the fig-trees for a month or two ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original: ἐπὶ τῶν κραδῶν ᾄδουσʼ , Ἀθηναῖοι δʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: whereas the Athenians spend their whole lives in chanting forth judgments from their law-courts . That is why we started off with a basket , a stew-pot and some myrtle boughs and have come to seek a quiet country

sections.label: Section 45
sections.original: ὅποι καθιδρυθέντε διαγενοίμεθʼ ἄν .
sections.translation: in which to settle . We are going to Tereus , the Epops , to learn from him , whether , in his aerial flights , he has noticed some town of this kind .

sections.label: Section 49
sections.original: οὗτος .
sections.translation: Here ! look !

sections.label: Section 49b
sections.original: τί ἔστιν ;
sections.translation: What ' s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 49c
sections.original: ἡ κορώνη μοι πάλαι
sections.translation: Why , the crow has been directing me

sections.label: Section 50
sections.original: ἄνω τι φράζει .
sections.translation: to something up there for some time now .

sections.label: Section 50b
sections.original: χὠ κολοιὸς οὑτοσὶ
sections.translation: And the jay is also opening it beak and craning its neck to show me I know not what . Clearly , there are some birds about here . We shall soon know , if we kick up a noise to start them .

sections.label: Section 54
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οἶσθʼ ὃ δρᾶσον ; τῷ σκέλει θένε τὴν πέτραν .
sections.translation: Do you know what to do ? Knock your leg against this rock .

sections.label: Section 55
sections.original: σὺ δὲ τῇ κεφαλῇ γʼ , ἵνʼ ᾖ διπλάσιος ὁ ψόφος .
sections.translation: And you your head to double the noise .

sections.label: Section 56
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὖν λίθῳ κόψον λαβών .
sections.translation: Well then use a stone instead ; take one and hammer with it .

sections.label: Section 56b
sections.original: πάνυ γʼ , εἰ δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: Good idea ! He does so . Ho there , within ! Slave ! slave !

sections.label: Section 57b
sections.original: τί λέγεις οὗτος ; τὸν ἔποπα παῖ καλεῖς ;
sections.translation: What ' s that , friend ! You say , slave , to summon Epops ? It would be much better to shout , Epops , Epops !

sections.label: Section 59
sections.original: ἐποποῖ . ποιήσεις ἔτι με κόπτειν αὖθις αὖ .
sections.translation: Well then , Epops ! Must I knock again ? Epops !

sections.label: Section 60b
sections.original: τίνες οὗτοι ; τίς ὁ βοῶν τὸν δεσπότην ;
sections.translation: Rushing out of a thicket . Who ' s there ? Who calls my master ?

sections.label: Section 61
sections.original: Ἄπολλον ἀποτρόπαιε τοῦ χασμήματος .
sections.translation: Apollo the Deliverer ! what an enormous beak !

sections.label: Section 62
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλας ὀρνιθοθήρα τουτωί .
sections.translation: Good god ! they are bird-catchers .

sections.label: Section 63
sections.original: οὕτως τι δεινὸν οὐδὲ κάλλιον λέγειν .
sections.translation: But is it so terrible ? Wouldn ' t it be better to explain things ?

sections.label: Section 64
sections.original: ἀπολεῖσθον .
sections.translation: You ' re done for .

sections.label: Section 64b
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐκ ἐσμὲν ἀνθρώπω .
sections.translation: But we are not men .

sections.label: Section 64c
sections.original: τί δαί ;
sections.translation: What are you , then ?

sections.label: Section 65
sections.original: Ὑποδεδιὼς ἔγωγε Λιβυκὸν ὄρνεον .
sections.translation: I am the Fearling , an African bird .

sections.label: Section 66
sections.original: οὐδὲν λέγεις .
sections.translation: You talk nonsense .

sections.label: Section 66b
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐροῦ τὰ πρὸς ποδῶν .
sections.translation: Well , then , just ask it of my feet .

sections.label: Section 67
sections.original: ὁδὶ δὲ δὴ τίς ἐστιν ὄρνις ; οὐκ ἐρεῖς ;
sections.translation: And this other one , what bird is it ? To Pisthetaerus . Speak up !

sections.label: Section 68
sections.original: Ἐπικεχοδὼς ἔγωγε Φασιανικός .
sections.translation: I ? I am a Crapple , from the land of the pheasants .

sections.label: Section 69
sections.original: ἀτὰρ σὺ τί θηρίον ποτʼ εἶ πρὸς τῶν θεῶν ;
sections.translation: But you yourself , in the name of the gods ! what animal are you ?

sections.label: Section 70
sections.original: ὄρνις ἔγωγε δοῦλος .
sections.translation: Why , I am a slave-bird .

sections.label: Section 70b
sections.original: ἡττήθης τινὸς
sections.translation: Why , have you been conquered by a cock ?

sections.label: Section 71b
sections.original: οὐκ ἀλλʼ ὅτε περ ὁ δεσπότης
sections.translation: No , but when my master was turned into a hoopoe , he begged me to become a bird also , to follow and to serve him .

sections.label: Section 74
sections.original: δεῖται γὰρ ὄρνις καὶ διακόνου τινός ;
sections.translation: Does a bird need a servant , then ?

sections.label: Section 75
sections.original: οὗτός γʼ , ἅτʼ οἶμαι πρότερον ἄνθρωπός ποτʼ ὤν ,
sections.translation: That ' s no doubt because he was once a man . At times he wants to eat a dish of sardines from Phalerum ; I seize my dish and fly to fetch him some . Again he wants some pea-soup ; I seize a ladle and a pot and run to get it .

sections.label: Section 79b
sections.original: τροχίλος ὄρνις οὑτοσί .
sections.translation: This is , then ,

sections.label: Section 80
sections.original: οἶσθʼ οὖν ὃ δρᾶσον ὦ τροχίλε ; τὸν δεσπότην
sections.translation: truly a running-bird . Come , Trochilus , do us the kindness to call your master .

sections.label: Section 81b
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἀρτίως νὴ τὸν Δία
sections.translation: Why , he has just fallen asleep after a feed of myrtle-berries and a few grubs .

sections.label: Section 83
sections.original: ὅμως ἐπέγειρον αὐτόν .
sections.translation: Never mind ; wake him up .

sections.label: Section 83b
sections.original: οἶδα μὲν σαφῶς
sections.translation: I am certain he will be angry . However , I will wake him to please you .

sections.label: Section 85
sections.original: κακῶς σύ γʼ ἀπόλοιʼ , ὥς μʼ ἀπέκτεινας δέει .
sections.translation: You cursed brute ! why , I am almost dead with terror !

sections.label: Section 86
sections.original: οἴμοι κακοδαίμων χὠ κολοιός μοἴχεται
sections.translation: Oh ! my god ! it was sheer fear that made me lose my jay .

sections.label: Section 87b
sections.original: ὦ δειλότατον σὺ θηρίον ,
sections.translation: Ah ! you big coward ! were you so frightened that you let go your jay ?
